summary: McKenzie announces the release of Bingo Card Creator version 1.04, distributed through Robosoft for automated submission to download sites. The update includes a Mac version, improved visual design with stock icons, and several user-requested features like font selection and single-card centering. He notes a practical tip about creating separate PAD files for Windows and Mac versions. The post reflects his early-stage microISV development process of iterating quickly based on customer feedback.
key_points: Bingo Card Creator 1.04 adds Mac support, expanding the potential customer base
Customer feedback directly drives feature development — a specific customer (Sally) requested single-card centering and got it
Automated submission tools like Robosoft save significant time when distributing to download sites
Practical PAD file tip: separate files for Windows and Mac versions simplifies submission
money_quotes: I love not having to submit to half a zillion places by myself
key_insight: Early-stage software businesses grow by responding directly to individual customer requests and systematizing distribution to maximize reach.
